payload.title: New York City Transit Key Performance Metrics

evidence_refs.source_quote: January 2025
prepared for the January 2025 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee
January 27, 2025

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.raw_text: Demetrius Crichlow President New York City Transit

evidence_refs.source_quote: Demetrius Crichlow President New York City Transit

tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City. Our 45,000 employees serve 4,500,000 passengers a day.

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City
The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network
Buses SVP Frank Annicaro
the NYPD continues to enforce laws, rules, and regulations within the transit system
NYPD Transit Bureau
Chair Lieber
23-member Board of Directors
the MTA Board awarded the contract to Kawasaki to construct up to 1,610 R211 cars

payload.title: New York City Transit project lead

evidence_refs.source_quote: Project Lead Hugo Zamora
Hugo Zamora will once again serve as the dedicated New York City Transit project lead

tool_args.raw_text: On January 17, the MTA commenced a major project to rebuild significant portions of the Rockaway Line, which connects both the A and Rockaway Shuttle trains between the Rockaway peninsula and Howard Beach.

payload.description: Major project to rebuild significant portions of the Rockaway Line connecting the A and Rockaway Shuttle trains between the Rockaway peninsula and Howard Beach. $393 million project to harden and upgrade vulnerable segments including the South Channel Bridge and Hammels Wye, and complete state of good repair work to track, stations, and electrical components.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On January 17, the MTA commenced a major project to rebuild significant portions of the Rockaway Line
this $393 million project will bring improvements to the entire line

tool_args.raw_text: this month we are seeking Board approval for the Queens Bus Network Redesign Proposed Final Plan Addendum. This proposed redesign aims to improve route design, connectivity, frequency, and accessibility. The plan will be implemented in two phases, scheduled for the summer and fall of 2025, pending Board approval.

payload.project_name: Queens Bus Network Redesign Proposed Final Plan Addendum
payload.description: Proposed redesign of Queens bus network aiming to improve route design, connectivity, frequency, and accessibility. Will be implemented in two phases, scheduled for summer and fall of 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: seeking Board approval for the Queens Bus Network Redesign Proposed Final Plan Addendum
implemented in two phases, scheduled for the summer and fall of 2025

payload.description: Fleet of up to 1,610 R211 subway cars being built by Kawasaki for NYCT. R211A debuted on the A line in March 2023, R211T (open-gangway) debuted on the C line in February 2024, R211S introduced on Staten Island Railway in fall 2024. 405 cars comprise majority of rolling stock on A and C lines. 20 R211T cars now in service. 80 more R211T open-gangway cars to come starting in 2027.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In January 2018, the MTA Board awarded the contract to Kawasaki to construct up to 1,610 R211 cars
The option exercised in December of last year means that 80 more R211T open-gangway cars will come to the system starting in 2027

tool_args.label: Zero-emission bus purchase announcement
tool_args.raw_text: This month Chair Lieber and Buses SVP Frank Annicaro were at Jamaica Depot to announce the purchase of 265 new zero-emission buses, the first project funded by congestion pricing.

payload.claim_text: This month Chair Lieber and Buses SVP Frank Annicaro were at Jamaica Depot to announce the purchase of 265 new zero-emission buses, the first project funded by congestion pricing.

evidence_refs.source_quote: This month Chair Lieber and Buses SVP Frank Annicaro were at Jamaica Depot to announce the purchase of 265 new zero-emission buses, the first project funded by congestion pricing.

tool_args.label: Queensboro Plaza accessibility completion
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA, community advocates, City Council Member Julie Won, and State Senator Kristen Gonzalez, celebrate the completion of accessibility improvements at Queensboro Plaza Station on December 12, 2024.

payload.event_name: Accessibility improvements completion at Queensboro Plaza Station
payload.description: Celebration of completion of accessibility improvements at Queensboro Plaza Station with MTA, community advocates, Council Member Julie Won, and State Senator Kristen Gonzalez

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA, community advocates, City Council Member Julie Won, and State Senator Kristen Gonzalez, celebrate the completion of accessibility improvements at Queensboro Plaza Station on December 12, 2024.

tool_args.label: Rockaway Line Rehabilitation commencement
tool_args.raw_text: On January 17, the MTA commenced a major project to rebuild significant portions of the Rockaway Line

payload.description: Commencement of the Rockaway Line Rehabilitation & Resiliency Project. 17-week project includes comprehensive repairs to Superstorm Sandy-damaged infrastructure. Buses providing free shuttle service on Q97 and Q109 routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On January 17, the MTA commenced a major project to rebuild significant portions of the Rockaway Line
the rebuild of the Rockaway A Line began on January 17

tool_args.label: R211 debut in revenue service
tool_args.raw_text: the first R211 train entered revenue service at Inwood-207 St on the A line on March 10, 2023

evidence_refs.source_quote: the first R211 train entered revenue service at Inwood-207 St on the A line on March 10, 2023

tool_args.label: R211T open-gangway debut
tool_args.raw_text: In February of 2024, the R211T debuted on the C line, the first open-gangway trains in the history of New York City Transit.

tool_args.label: Subway weekday OTP Dec 2024
tool_args.raw_text: For December 2024, weekday On-Time Performance (OTP) was 80.4%, a 0.7% improvement from December 2023.

evidence_refs.source_quote: weekday On-Time Performance (OTP) was 80.4%, a 0.7% improvement from December 2023

tool_args.label: Subway weekend OTP Dec 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Weekend OTP was 84.7%.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Weekend OTP was 84.7%

tool_args.label: Subway CJTP Dec 2024
tool_args.raw_text: The percentage of customers arriving at their destinations within five minutes of schedule (CJTP) was 83.9%, a 1.0% improvement from December 2023.
